question_text: A student makes an electromagnet using a battery, wire coiled around an iron nail, and a switch. Which change would make the electromagnet stronger?

options.label: A
options.text: Use a thinner wire to reduce resistance

options.label: B
options.text: Use a battery with a higher voltage

options.label: C
options.text: Use fewer coils of wire around the nail

options.label: D
options.text: Use a plastic rod instead of an iron nail

correct_answer: B) Use a battery with a higher voltage
explanation: A higher voltage increases the current flowing through the wire, which creates a stronger magnetic field. Using fewer coils or a non-magnetic core would weaken the electromagnet, and thinner wire would actually increase resistance.
